component.name: Container
component.description: sbomify's production container image. Justifications for High advisories embedded in the osv-scanner and cosign CLI binaries the OSV and signature-verification plugins invoke. sbomify treats the inputs these tools process (user-uploaded SBOMs, downloaded signature bundles) as untrusted; each justification rests on code-path unreachability, not trust.

vulnerabilities.description: Moby/Docker daemon authorization-plugin (AuthZ) bypass via an oversized request body, in github.com/docker/docker/pkg/authorization. Embedded as an indirect Go-module dependency of osv-scanner. Fixed in Docker Engine 29.3.1.

analysis.detail: The vulnerable code is the Docker daemon's server-side AuthZ middleware. docker/docker is an indirect dependency of osv-scanner (via osv-scalibr / go-containerregistry) used only by its container-image scanning paths, which act as a client. osv-scanner never runs a Docker daemon, so the path is unreachable; sbomify uses osv-scanner only for static SBOM/dependency analysis.

vulnerabilities.description: Docker/Moby daemon archive-copy handler (PUT /containers/{id}/archive, `docker cp`) resolves a decompression binary (xz/unpigz) from the running container's filesystem, so a trojanised in-container decompressor can execute with daemon privileges. Embedded as an indirect Go-module dependency of osv-scanner. Fixed in Docker Engine 29.5.1 / moby v2.0.0-beta.14.

analysis.detail: A Docker daemon runtime code path (the archive-upload handler spawning an external in-container decompressor), not an image-layer/tar parsing bug. osv-scanner does static dependency/SBOM analysis and never starts a daemon, creates a container, or invokes the archive-copy path, so the function is unreachable in sbomify.

vulnerabilities.description: Race condition (TOCTOU) in the Docker daemon's `docker cp` mount setup (github.com/docker/docker/daemon): a process inside a container can replace the bind-mount destination with a symlink, redirecting the mount to an arbitrary host path. Embedded as an indirect Go-module dependency of osv-scanner. Fixed in Docker Engine 29.5.1 / moby v2.0.0-beta.14.

analysis.detail: Daemon-only TOCTOU mount-setup code reached via `docker cp` / PUT|HEAD /containers/{id}/archive against a live daemon. osv-scanner parses lockfiles/SBOMs and image-layer metadata and never executes the daemon's copy/mount logic, so the path is unreachable in sbomify.

vulnerabilities.description: Go standard library `mime` package: quadratic-complexity CPU exhaustion (DoS) in mime.WordDecoder.DecodeHeader when decoding an RFC 2047 MIME header with many invalid encoded-words. Present because the cosign v3.1.1 binary is built with Go 1.26.3 (fixed in Go 1.25.11 / 1.26.4).

analysis.detail: mime.WordDecoder.DecodeHeader is the RFC 2047 email encoded-word decoder; it is not on cosign's execution path. cosign's communication with the OCI registry, Rekor, and Fulcio is HTTP+JSON and never decodes RFC 2047 email headers, so the function -- compiled into the binary's stdlib -- is unreachable. cosign v3.1.1 is the latest release; sbomify rebuilds on the next cosign release compiled with Go 1.26.4+.
